metadata.title: Quick-fire: trust, after the fact
metadata.author: patterns-of-choice editorial draft (AI)
metadata.design_intent: Holds the abstract (de-attached) twins for H8 reciprocity paired probes pp-reciprocity-001 and -002 (items 1-2), plus two same-domain trust/forgiveness-vs-vigilance siblings (items 3-4) to form a complete quick-fire. Twins support D = r_narr - r_abs per scoring.md §9.1.
metadata.domain_anchor: trust axis (trust to vigilance) + forgiveness; the abstract pole of the H8 reciprocity probes and ordinary same-construct siblings.

items.prompt: Someone you used to work with once let you down on a shared task — they took the visible credit for work that was mostly yours, and never made it right at the time. Lately they've clearly changed: they've owned up to it, unprompted, and have been straight with you since. Now they ask to split a new (small, low-stakes) shared task the same way as before — you do the substantive half, they handle the visible half and represent it to others. Do you:

options.text: Take them at their word and rely on them to carry their half, exposed the same way you were last time.
Go along but keep yourself covered — stay attached to the visible part so you're not exposed if they fall through again.

items.prompt: An ongoing, casual mutual arrangement with a close friend (you each cover it when you can, nobody counts closely) has quietly drifted lopsided — you've been carrying clearly more than half for a while. It has never been said out loud, and the unspoken ease is part of why the friendship feels effortless. You do:

options.text: Keep carrying it without saying anything; you'd rather absorb the difference than turn an easy thing into accounting.
Name the imbalance and ask to rebalance it, even though making the implicit explicit risks the frictionless reciprocity you both rely on.

items.prompt: A teammate proposes splitting an unfamiliar task by trust — you each take half and don't check each other's work. It would be faster, but if they slip, your name is on it too.

options.text: Take the split on trust; assume they'll pull their weight.
Ask to review each other's halves before it ships.

items.prompt: A neighbor who flaked on a shared plan last month asks for a small favor today. Helping costs you little; they may or may not return it.

options.text: Help anyway — keep your side of the street clean regardless of theirs.
Politely pass; you'd rather not keep fronting effort that doesn't come back.
